title: Hammeria meleagris
taxonomy: Hammeria meleagris is a genus of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. This succulent is native to South Africa, specifically in the Western Cape Province. Hammeria meleagris is classified within the plant family Aizoaceae, which consists of leaf-stem succulents commonly known as 'living stones'. Within the Aizoaceae family, Hammeria meleagris is further classified within the subfamily Ruschioideae. The classification of Hammeria meleagris is still a subject of ongoing botanical research and may be subject to revision in the future.

intro: Hammeria meleagris, also known as African Hammeria, is a plant species native to Africa, belonging to the family Hammeriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hammeria meleagris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hammeria meleagris is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
